title: Manjarabad Fort
tagline: Manjarabad Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Manjarabad fort is a star fort built in 1792 by Tipu Sultan, ruling on behalf of the king of Mysore following French star-shaped fort designs made popular by Sébastien Le Prestre d
overview.description: Manjarabad fort is a star fort built in 1792 by Tipu Sultan, ruling on behalf of the king of Mysore following French star-shaped fort designs made popular by Sébastien Le Prestre de Vauban that deflect cannon fire and allow guns to cover all approaches without dead positions. It is in the Hassan district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Donigal
topPlaces.description: Donigal is a village in Hassan district of Karnataka state, India. Donigal village is nestled in the Western Ghats forests of India.

topPlaces.name: Sakleshpur
topPlaces.description: Sakleshpur, also known as Sakleshpura or Sakaleshapura, is a hill station town and headquarters of Sakleshpur taluk and one of the two Sub-divisional headquarters in Hassan district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is known as the Switzerland of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Arekeri
topPlaces.description: Arakeri is a small village in Bijapur district, Karnataka, India. It is around 14 km from Bijapur city, located near to the Solapur, Mangalore highway.

topPlaces.name: Achangi
topPlaces.description: Achangi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sakleshpur taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Green Route
topPlaces.description: Green Route is a segment along the Bangalore and Mangalore railway line in India, within the Western Ghat mountain ranges. This is the railway track from Sakaleshpura (altitude 906 m MSL) to the Kukke Subramanya (altitude 120 m MSL) Road station. It is 52 km long, with around 57 tunnels and 109 bridges of length varying from few metres to 0.75 km and height varying from a few to 200 metres.

topPlaces.name: Agalahatti
topPlaces.description: Agalahatti is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sakleshpur taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Manjarabad Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Manjarabad Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Manjarabad Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (99 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Manjarabad Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Manjarabad Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Manjarabad Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Manjarabad Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Manjarabad Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Donigal, Sakleshpur, Arekeri, Achangi, Green Route.
